project_type: Node.js distributed task queue engine (monorepo with shared/, server/, worker/)
language: TypeScript (ESM, strict mode with noUncheckedIndexedAccess and exactOptionalPropertyTypes)
build_system: npm workspaces - must build shared first (others depend on its dist/)
test_framework: Vitest with ioredis-mock for Redis and manual pg Pool mock. Tests in tests/ directory at root.
verification_instructions: 1. Run npm run build to compile all workspaces. 2. Run npm test to run Vitest suite. Both must exit 0.
setup_instructions: npm install (already done). No Docker/Redis/Postgres needed for tests - all mocked.
environment_constraints: No persistent services (Redis/Postgres) available. Tests use ioredis-mock and mock pg Pool. ioredis-mock does NOT support BRPOP/BLMOVE - test worker logic via processOne() directly. ESM modules with type: module.
contribution_requirements: Keep Phase 1 tests passing. Use strict TypeScript. Follow existing patterns.
key_patterns: Redis keys centralized in shared/src/keys.ts. DB operations as thin functions in shared/src/db.ts exported from shared/src/index.ts. Worker uses processOne() for per-job logic (testable). Producer uses redis.multi() for atomic Redis writes. Types in shared/src/types.ts. Tests mock pg with vi.fn() capturing queries array.
directory_structure: shared/ - types, keys, db helpers, redis client factory. server/ - Express REST API + Producer SDK. worker/ - Worker pull loop + handler registry. tests/ - Vitest test files. infra/postgres/ - SQL migration files. examples/ - smoke test script.
